source: Self-supervised Learning for Large-scale Item Recommendations

concepts.finding: Self-supervised graph learning for recommendation. Contrastive learning on user-item graph: node dropout, edge dropout, random walk augmentation. Maximizes agreement between differently augmented views of same node. Outperforms supervised baselines on recommendation tasks. Data augmentation provides robustness.
concepts.implication: Self-supervised keyword embedding via graph contrastive learning: augment keyword graph (drop edges, mask nodes) and maximize agreement between augmented views. No labels needed. Edge dropout prevents overfitting to noisy co-occurrences.
concepts.open_question: What data augmentations (edge dropout, feature masking, node dropout) work best for keyword co-occurrence graphs?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Self-supervised contrastive learning on keyword co-occurrence graphs (without labels) produces intent clusters competitive with supervised methods.
